title: The docs sweep
why_fits: Use this when code ships faster than docs can follow; the PR artifact keeps the review human without requiring a human to hunt for gaps.

title: The architecture satisfaction loop
why_fits: Reach for this when a refactor has a clear destination state but you need the agent to self-verify each step before moving on.

title: The sub-50 ms page-load loop
why_fits: Best when performance has a hard numeric gate; the fixed harness prevents the agent from declaring victory on a single lucky run.

title: The production error sweep
why_fits: Pairs well with a nightly cron; the clean-stop clause prevents noisy runs when logs are healthy.

title: The 100% test coverage loop
why_fits: Use when a project mandates a coverage threshold; the risk-first ordering keeps the agent from filling easy lines while hard paths stay dark.

title: The SEO/GEO visibility loop
why_fits: Reach for this when you need a repeatable visibility benchmark rather than a one-time audit; the fixed query set makes progress measurable.

title: The logging coverage loop
why_fits: Treats observability as a first-class test requirement; the redaction check makes it safe to run on paths that touch sensitive data.

title: The nightly changelog loop
why_fits: Automates the part of release communication that most teams skip; the user-relevance filter keeps it from becoming a raw commit feed.

title: The quality streak loop
why_fits: Use when intermittent failures matter as much as average quality; the streak restart means isolated lucky runs cannot satisfy the bar.

title: The full product evaluation loop
why_fits: The capability map and final full-rerun catch fixes that trade one scenario's quality for another; good for pre-launch gates.

title: The test-suite speed loop
why_fits: Reach for this when slow CI is the bottleneck; the before/after timing baseline stops speed gains from hiding coverage regressions.

title: The repository cleanup loop
why_fits: The classify-then-recover-then-delete order prevents accidental loss; use periodically on active repos before each major release.

title: The stale-safe batch release loop
why_fits: Eliminates the most common cause of silent regressions in batch deploys; the integrated-main rule means the artifact you ship is exactly what was reviewed.

title: The production data cleanup loop
why_fits: Fixes both the current records and the classifier in one pass; the explicit inclusion/exclusion rules make future cleanup decisions auditable.

title: The post-release baseline loop
why_fits: Without this, future perf comparisons have no anchored reference; run it as a mandatory post-deploy step on every versioned release.

title: The ticket-to-PR-ready loop
why_fits: Turns a vague ticket into a bounded, evidence-backed patch; the structured handoff removes detective work from the reviewer's plate.

title: The customer AI deployment loop
why_fits: The approval-gate and gradual-rollout pattern makes it safe to hand off AI workflow delivery to an agent while keeping humans in the critical path.

title: The product update podcast loop
why_fits: Source verification and the no-episode clause prevent the agent from generating content when nothing meaningful shipped.

title: The Clodex adversarial-review loop
why_fits: Two-model separation (Claude builds, Codex reviews) catches the blind spots a single-model loop will rationalize away.

title: The Loop Harness verification loop
why_fits: The worktree isolation and second-agent gate make it appropriate for supervised recurring checks where one agent should not both generate and approve output.

title: The Boeing 747 benchmark
why_fits: The nine-angle rig adapts to any rendered subject; use it as a concrete evaluation harness whenever you need repeatable visual quality measurement.

title: War Loops: frontend reconstruction
why_fits: The static/motion/responsive three-axis split makes it harder for the agent to declare fidelity on appearance alone while motion or reflow are broken.

title: The self-improving champion loop
why_fits: The holdout gate prevents Goodhart effects; each round genuinely has to beat a fresh sample, not the same training examples.

title: The devil's-advocate loop
why_fits: Best used before committing to a consequential design; the persistent objection log stops the builder from closing issues with explanation rather than evidence.

title: The fresh-clone loop
why_fits: Destroying the environment after each fix is the only way to prove the README is sufficient; local state will always mask the next hidden assumption.

title: The Infinite Clickbait thumbnail loop
why_fits: The accuracy gate keeps curiosity-driven concepts from becoming misleading thumbnails; the fixed rubric makes round-over-round scores comparable.

title: The autonomy-loop builder-reviewer loop
why_fits: The revert-or-mutate proof requirement catches tests that pass without actually asserting the fix; useful when you need high confidence in automated patches.

title: The Codex completion-contract loop
why_fits: Prevents long-running agent sessions from declaring done when only some requirements are met; the evidence table keeps partial state visible.

title: The Revolve versioned-experiment loop
why_fits: Revision boundaries stop experiments run under different rubrics from being compared as equivalent; critical for honest long-running optimization.

title: The five-minute repository maintainer loop
why_fits: One thread per repo and the proof-before-land gate make multi-repo agent orchestration steerable without requiring constant human input.

title: The recent-feedback sweep
why_fits: Turns one-off user corrections into a reusable audit rubric; the fresh full-sweep at the end verifies current state rather than trusting old thread assertions.

title: Prompt chaining (Anthropic building-effective-agents)
why_fits: Reach for this whenever a task naturally decomposes into ordered subtasks where each step's output gates the next; the clearest entry point for agentic work.

title: Routing (Anthropic building-effective-agents)
why_fits: Use when inputs vary enough that a single prompt or subagent cannot handle them all well; classification before action reduces hallucination on edge cases.

title: Parallelization (Anthropic building-effective-agents)
why_fits: Use sectioning for throughput and voting for confidence; the majority-vote variant is a lightweight adversarial check without needing a dedicated critic agent.

title: Orchestrator-subagents (Anthropic building-effective-agents)
why_fits: The central orchestrator pattern keeps complex multi-step work steerable; use when no single subagent can see the full context needed to sequence the task.

title: Evaluator-optimizer (Anthropic building-effective-agents)
why_fits: The generate-then-evaluate loop is the simplest path to quality improvement when you can articulate what good looks like as a separate critique prompt.

title: Human-in-the-loop checkpointing (LangGraph)
why_fits: Use when an agentic loop must pause at risky or irreversible actions and resume only after human approval; the persistent state means no work is lost on interrupt.

title: Multi-agent supervisor pattern (LangGraph)
why_fits: The supervisor routes tasks to specialized workers and aggregates results; better than a monolith when subtasks require different tools or prompts.

title: Reflection agent pattern (LangGraph)
why_fits: A fast way to add iterative quality improvement to any text-generation task without building a separate critic agent; works well when speed matters more than diversity.
